title: headers.rs: Parse functions panic on malformed payload_length field
description:
File: rust/lichen-schc/src/headers.rs
Lines: 338-339, 514-515, 641, 776

Category: CORRECTNESS

All parse functions read the IPv6 payload_length field and use it to slice the packet without verifying it does not exceed actual packet length.

Example in parse_coap_udp (lines 338-339):
```rust
let payload_length = u16::from_be_bytes([raw[4], raw[5]]) as usize;
let udp = &raw[IPV6_HEADER_LEN..IPV6_HEADER_LEN + payload_length];
```

If a malformed packet has payload_length=500 but raw.len()=52, slicing raw[40..540] will panic.

Impact: A malformed/truncated packet causes panic instead of returning an error.

Fix: Add validation after reading payload_length:
```rust
if IPV6_HEADER_LEN + payload_length > raw.len() {
    return Err(PacketError::TooShort { ... });
}
```
